task_completion.description: Navigate to the correct dot-connecting activity in GCompris.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GCompris application is open and the 'Draw numbers' activity (located in Mathematics > Numeracy) is active on the screen.

task_completion.description: Solve the puzzle by connecting the dots in sequence to reveal the hidden picture.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The numbered dots in the GCompris activity are connected in numerical order, revealing a completed drawing of a number (0-9).

task_completion.description: Save a screenshot of the revealed picture to '~/Documents/solved_dots.png'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or image saving dialog shows a file named 'solved_dots.png' located in the '/home/ga/Documents' directory.
A text editor, file manager, or terminal shows a file named 'object_id.txt' located in the '/home/ga/Documents' directory.

task_completion.description: Write a sentence identifying the revealed object in the text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of 'object_id.txt' are visible on screen and contain a sentence that correctly identifies the specific number (0-9) drawn in the puzzle.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GCompris application to solve the puzzle.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GCompris GUI being actively used (e.g., mouse cursor clicking dots), rather than the agent just downloading a pre-solved image from the internet.

integrity.description: The saved screenshot is a genuine capture of the completed puzzle.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image saved as 'solved_dots.png' should visually match the GCompris interface and the specific puzzle solved during the trajectory.

integrity.description: The agent visually identified the object rather than using a hardcoded script.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in 'object_id.txt' should correspond to the actual number revealed in the GCompris session shown in earlier screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target GCompris activity is actually called 'Draw numbers' (located in the Mathematics > Numeracy category), not 'Connect the dots'. The puzzle requires clicking numbered dots in sequence to draw numbers 0-9. The expected file paths are exactly /home/ga/Documents/solved_dots.png and /home/ga/Documents/object_id.txt. Scoring is distributed as: navigation (30 pts), completion (30 pts), screenshot file evidence (15 pts), text file evidence (15 pts), and content check (10 pts).
